goal: Generate a narrated podcast episode from a topic prompt
description: Gemini writes a conversational podcast script from a topic, ElevenLabs converts it to realistic speech audio, and the episode metadata is stored in Notion.

steps.action: Generate a podcast script with intro, segments, and outro from the given topic
steps.error_hint: Gemini may refuse topics it considers sensitive — rephrase or add context to the prompt

steps.action: Convert the script text to speech audio using a selected voice
steps.error_hint: Scripts over 5000 characters must be chunked — ElevenLabs has per-request character limits

steps.action: Create a Notion page with episode metadata, script, and audio link
steps.error_hint: Notion blocks have a 2000-character limit per block — split long scripts into multiple blocks

gotchas: ElevenLabs character limits don't match Gemini's output length — always chunk the script before sending to TTS
Audio URLs from ElevenLabs are temporary; download and store the file before the link expires

goal: Natural language to SQL query execution with results reported to Slack
description: Mistral translates a natural language question into SQL, Neon DB executes the query, and results are formatted and posted to a Slack channel.

steps.action: Generate a SQL query from the user's natural language question using the provided schema context
steps.error_hint: Always pass the full DDL schema as context — Mistral will hallucinate table names without it

steps.action: Execute the generated SQL query against the Neon Postgres database
steps.error_hint: Add a LIMIT clause if the LLM omits one — unbounded queries can time out on serverless Neon

steps.action: Post a formatted table of query results to the specified Slack channel
steps.error_hint: Slack messages have a 4000-character limit — truncate large result sets and add a 'view full results' link

gotchas: Never execute DELETE/DROP/UPDATE queries generated by the LLM without a confirmation step — add a read-only guard
Neon serverless may cold-start on first query; allow a 3-5 second timeout buffer

goal: Automate competitive intelligence gathering into a structured knowledge base
description: Perplexity researches competitors and market trends, stores structured findings in a Notion database, and sends a digest summary to Slack.

steps.action: Research the specified competitor or market topic and return structured findings with citations
steps.error_hint: Perplexity may return outdated info for fast-moving topics — include a date range filter in the query

steps.action: Create or update a Notion database entry with structured competitive intel
steps.error_hint: Notion API rate limit is 3 requests/second — batch multiple property updates into a single call

steps.action: Send a competitive intelligence digest to the strategy channel
steps.error_hint: Use Slack Block Kit for rich formatting — plain text digests are hard to scan

gotchas: Perplexity citations may include paywalled sources that your team can't access — flag these in the Notion entry
Run deduplication against existing Notion entries before creating new pages to avoid duplicate intel records

goal: Deploy to both Vercel and Netlify from a single GitHub push
description: A GitHub push triggers GitHub Actions, which runs tests and then deploys in parallel to both Vercel and Netlify, reporting status back.

steps.action: Trigger CI workflow on push: run lint, type-check, and test suite
steps.error_hint: Ensure the workflow YAML uses 'on: push' with the correct branch filter — mismatched branches silently skip

steps.action: Deploy the build artifact to Vercel and capture the preview URL
steps.error_hint: Vercel has a 50MB serverless function size limit — large dependencies cause silent deploy failures

steps.action: Deploy the build artifact to Netlify and capture the preview URL
steps.error_hint: Netlify redirects require a _redirects file in the publish directory — SPA routing will break without it

steps.action: Post deployment summary with both preview URLs to the team channel
steps.error_hint: Include the commit SHA in the message so the team can trace which code version is deployed

gotchas: Vercel and Netlify have different environment variable formats — secrets must be configured separately in each platform
Steps 2 and 3 can run in parallel, but both depend on step 1 passing — add proper failure handling if CI fails

goal: Create an invoice in FreshBooks with an attached Stripe payment link and email receipt
description: FreshBooks generates an invoice, Stripe creates a hosted payment link for it, and Postmark sends the invoice email with the payment link to the client.

steps.action: Create an invoice with line items and client details
steps.error_hint: FreshBooks requires the client to exist first — create the client record before generating the invoice

steps.action: Create a Stripe Payment Link for the invoice amount
steps.error_hint: Stripe amounts are in the smallest currency unit (cents) — multiply FreshBooks total by 100 for USD

steps.action: Send the invoice email with the Stripe payment link to the client
steps.error_hint: Postmark requires a verified sender signature — emails will bounce if the From address isn't verified

gotchas: Currency mismatch between FreshBooks (decimal) and Stripe (integer cents) is the #1 cause of wrong payment amounts
Set up a Stripe webhook to mark the FreshBooks invoice as paid when payment succeeds — this recipe doesn't close the loop automatically

goal: Enable Buy Now Pay Later checkout for BigCommerce orders
description: A BigCommerce order triggers a Klarna BNPL session, and a webhook confirms the payment status back to the store and notifies the team.

steps.action: Capture the order details from the e-commerce platform order event
steps.error_hint: Ensure order amounts include tax and shipping — partial amounts will cause Klarna session mismatches

steps.action: Create a Klarna BNPL checkout session for the order
steps.error_hint: Klarna requires line item totals to exactly match the order total — rounding errors will reject the session

steps.action: Notify the fulfillment team about the pending BNPL order
steps.error_hint: Don't trigger fulfillment from this notification — wait for the Klarna authorized webhook before shipping

gotchas: Klarna sessions expire in 48 hours — implement a cleanup job for abandoned BNPL checkouts
Klarna's order amount validation is strict to the cent; any mismatch between line items sum and total will fail

goal: Automate international hiring from offer acceptance to HR system onboarding
description: When a Greenhouse offer is accepted, Deel generates an international employment contract, and BambooHR creates the employee record for onboarding.

steps.action: Capture the accepted offer details including candidate info and compensation
steps.error_hint: Greenhouse webhooks deliver candidate_id not full data — you must call the Candidates API to get details

steps.action: Create an international employment contract via Deel EOR
steps.error_hint: Deel requires country-specific mandatory benefits — some countries need 13th month pay or pension configured

steps.action: Create an employee record in BambooHR with the new hire's details
steps.error_hint: BambooHR custom fields for Deel contract ID must be pre-created — the API will silently ignore unknown fields

gotchas: Deel contract creation can take up to 24 hours for some countries due to compliance checks — don't assume instant availability
Greenhouse offer status must be 'accepted' not 'created' — triggering on the wrong webhook event sends contracts prematurely

goal: Capture leads from a Webflow site via Typeform into a marketing CRM
description: A Typeform embedded in a Webflow site captures lead information, which flows into ActiveCampaign for nurture sequence enrollment.

steps.action: Receive and parse the completed Typeform submission
steps.error_hint: Typeform hidden fields must be configured to pass UTM params from the Webflow URL — they don't flow automatically

steps.action: Create or update a contact in ActiveCampaign and add to a nurture automation
steps.error_hint: ActiveCampaign custom fields must be created in the UI before the API can set them — unknown fields are silently dropped

steps.action: Notify the sales team about the new lead in real time
steps.error_hint: Avoid posting PII like phone numbers in public Slack channels — use a private channel or DM the assigned rep

gotchas: Webflow's Typeform embed uses an iframe — UTM parameters from the page URL must be explicitly passed via hidden fields
ActiveCampaign deduplicates by email — if the contact already exists, new custom field values will overwrite, not append

goal: Aggregate YouTube channel analytics into BigQuery for weekly Slack reporting
description: YouTube API pulls video performance metrics, BigQuery stores and aggregates them, and a formatted weekly summary is posted to Slack.

steps.action: Fetch video analytics for the channel over the past 7 days
steps.error_hint: YouTube Analytics API has a 2-day data delay — set date_range_end to 2 days ago, not today

steps.action: Insert the weekly metrics into the analytics table and run the aggregation query
steps.error_hint: BigQuery streaming inserts have a brief delay before data is queryable — add a short wait before the aggregation query

steps.action: Post the weekly YouTube analytics summary to the content team channel
steps.error_hint: Format large numbers with commas for readability — raw integers in Slack messages are hard to scan

gotchas: YouTube API quotas are 10,000 units/day — fetching analytics for many videos can exhaust the quota quickly
BigQuery table schema must match the YouTube API response shape — add new columns when YouTube adds new metrics

goal: Track Twitter engagement events in Segment for downstream analytics
description: Twitter API captures engagement data (likes, retweets, replies), Segment normalizes and routes the events, enabling downstream analytics tools to process social metrics.

steps.action: Fetch recent engagement metrics for specified tweets or account
steps.error_hint: Twitter API v2 rate limits are strict (300 requests/15min for app auth) — batch tweet IDs in a single request

steps.action: Send normalized engagement events to Segment for routing
steps.error_hint: Segment's track call requires a userId or anonymousId — use the Twitter account ID as the userId

steps.action: Post a social engagement summary to the marketing channel
steps.error_hint: Include the time period in the Slack message — engagement rates without context are meaningless

gotchas: Twitter API v2 returns different metric types for organic vs promoted tweets — ensure the Segment schema handles both
Segment event names must be consistent (e.g., 'Twitter Engagement') — inconsistent naming breaks downstream analytics funnels

goal: Scrape competitor pages and generate fast AI summaries
description: Firecrawl scrapes competitor web pages, Groq rapidly summarizes the content, and structured summaries are stored in Notion for the team.

steps.action: Scrape the target URLs and extract clean text content
steps.error_hint: Some sites block Firecrawl's default user-agent — use the stealth mode option for protected pages

steps.action: Summarize each scraped page using Groq's fast inference
steps.error_hint: Groq has a context window limit — truncate scraped pages to 6000 tokens before sending

steps.action: Store the summaries as entries in a competitive intelligence Notion database
steps.error_hint: Notion API rate limit is 3 req/sec — use sequential creates with a delay when adding multiple pages

gotchas: Firecrawl output can be massive for pages with lots of boilerplate — use the extract_schema option to target specific content
Groq's speed advantage is lost if you batch too many pages in one call — process pages individually for best latency

goal: Alert the team when a Databricks data pipeline job fails
description: Monitors Databricks job runs for failures, parses the error details, and sends a structured alert to Slack with debugging context.

steps.action: Check the latest job run status and capture error details if failed
steps.error_hint: Use the Runs List API with active_only=false to catch completed failures — active_only misses finished failed runs

steps.action: Parse and classify the error message into a human-readable diagnosis

input_mapping.system_prompt: Classify this Databricks error and suggest likely fixes

steps.error_hint: Some Databricks errors include stack traces over 10K characters — truncate to the first 2000 before sending to the LLM

steps.action: Send a structured failure alert with diagnosis to the data engineering channel
steps.error_hint: Use Slack message color coding — red for critical severity, yellow for warning — to help triage at a glance

gotchas: Databricks job webhooks may fire before the error message is fully populated — add a short delay before fetching run details
Don't send alerts for expected maintenance failures — filter by error type to avoid alert fatigue

goal: Build a semantic search pipeline with embedding and reranking
description: Documents are embedded with Cohere, stored in Pinecone, and search queries use Cohere reranking for high-precision results.

steps.action: Generate embeddings for the input documents or search query
steps.error_hint: Use input_type='search_document' for indexing and 'search_query' for queries — mixing them degrades relevance

steps.action: Upsert document embeddings or query the index for nearest neighbors
steps.error_hint: Pinecone index dimensions must match Cohere model output dimensions (1024 for embed-v3) — dimension mismatch causes upsert failure

steps.action: Rerank the Pinecone results using Cohere reranker for improved precision
steps.error_hint: Cohere reranker expects raw text, not embeddings — pass the document text from Pinecone metadata, not the vectors

gotchas: Cohere embed-v3 and embed-v2 produce different dimension vectors — mixing models in the same Pinecone index corrupts results
Store the original document text in Pinecone metadata so the reranker can access it without a separate database lookup

goal: Automatically invalidate Redis cache when PostgreSQL data changes
description: Listens for PostgreSQL change events, identifies affected cache keys in Redis, invalidates them, and logs the operation.

steps.action: Detect a data change event via LISTEN/NOTIFY or logical replication
steps.error_hint: PostgreSQL NOTIFY payloads are limited to 8000 bytes — for large rows, send only the row ID and fetch details separately

steps.action: Invalidate all cache keys matching the changed entity pattern
steps.error_hint: Avoid KEYS command in production Redis — use SCAN for pattern matching to prevent blocking the server

steps.action: Log the cache invalidation event to an ops channel for audit
steps.error_hint: Only log invalidations above a threshold (e.g., >10 keys) to avoid flooding the ops channel

gotchas: Cache key naming conventions must be consistent between the app and this pipeline — document the pattern (e.g., table:row_id:field)
Bulk UPDATE statements can trigger thousands of invalidation events — add debouncing or batching to prevent Redis overload

goal: Deploy Turso embedded replicas at the edge with Fly.io
description: Turso creates an embedded replica database, Fly.io deploys it to edge regions, and a health check verifies replication lag.

steps.action: Create an embedded replica database for the target edge region
steps.error_hint: Turso free tier allows only 3 databases — check quota before creating replicas

steps.action: Deploy the application with the Turso embedded replica to the specified Fly.io region
steps.error_hint: Fly.io region codes (e.g., 'nrt', 'iad') don't match Turso region names — maintain a mapping table

steps.action: Report the edge deployment status and replication health
steps.error_hint: Never post auth tokens to Slack — redact sensitive connection details from the notification

gotchas: Turso embedded replicas are read-only — writes must go to the primary database URL, not the replica
Fly.io and Turso region names differ — 'nrt' in Fly.io is 'tokyo' in Turso; mismatches defeat the latency benefit

goal: Sync ClickUp tasks with GitHub issues bidirectionally
description: When a ClickUp task is created, a corresponding GitHub issue is opened, and status changes sync back to ClickUp.

steps.action: Capture the new or updated ClickUp task details
steps.error_hint: ClickUp webhook payloads vary by event type — validate the event type before processing to avoid duplicate creates

steps.action: Create or update a GitHub issue matching the ClickUp task
steps.error_hint: GitHub assignees must be valid GitHub usernames — ClickUp display names won't match; maintain a name mapping

steps.action: Notify the dev channel about the new synced issue
steps.error_hint: Thread status update notifications under the original message to keep the channel clean

gotchas: Bidirectional sync can create infinite loops — add a 'synced-from-github' tag to prevent re-triggering the ClickUp webhook
ClickUp status names are custom per workspace — map them to GitHub issue labels explicitly

goal: Handle event registrations with confirmation and reminder emails
description: Typeform collects event registrations, Brevo sends a confirmation email immediately and schedules a reminder before the event.

steps.action: Receive and parse the event registration form submission
steps.error_hint: Typeform date fields return ISO 8601 strings — parse them before passing to Brevo's scheduling API

steps.action: Send a confirmation email and add the registrant to the event contact list
steps.error_hint: Brevo transactional email templates must be activated before use — draft templates return a 400 error

steps.action: Schedule a reminder email 24 hours before the event
steps.error_hint: Brevo scheduled sends use UTC — calculate the offset from the event timezone, not the server timezone

gotchas: Brevo's scheduling API requires the scheduled_at time to be at least 1 hour in the future — same-day events will fail the reminder
Typeform does not deduplicate submissions — add email-based dedup logic to prevent double registration

goal: Recommend the best credit card for a user's purchase via messaging
description: A user asks which card to use at a specific store, Card Navi analyzes their cards and the merchant, and sends a personalized recommendation via messaging.

steps.action: Analyze the user's card portfolio against the specified merchant for optimal rewards
steps.error_hint: Merchant name matching is fuzzy — normalize store names (e.g., 'Starbucks Coffee' vs 'STARBUCKS') before lookup

steps.action: Send the card recommendation to the user via direct message
steps.error_hint: Format the reward rate as a percentage and points as whole numbers — raw decimals confuse users

gotchas: Card reward categories change quarterly for some issuers — ensure Card Navi data is updated with current quarter's bonus categories
Some merchants map to unexpected MCC codes (e.g., grocery store gas stations) — the recommended card may differ from what users expect

goal: Sync frequently asked Help Scout questions to a Notion knowledge base
description: Help Scout conversations tagged as common questions are extracted, formatted, and synced to a Notion knowledge base for self-service.

steps.action: Fetch conversations tagged as FAQ candidates with their resolution threads
steps.error_hint: Help Scout API paginates at 50 items — implement pagination to capture all matching conversations

steps.action: Clean and structure the Q&A pairs into knowledge base article format

input_mapping.system_prompt: Format these support Q&As into clean knowledge base articles with title, summary, and detailed answer

steps.error_hint: Support threads contain internal notes and customer PII — strip these before sending to the LLM

steps.action: Create or update knowledge base articles in the Notion database
steps.error_hint: Search for existing articles by title before creating — duplicate articles fragment the knowledge base

gotchas: Help Scout conversation threads include internal notes — filter by thread type to exclude staff-only content before syncing
Notion database properties must include a 'source' field to track which articles came from Help Scout vs manual creation

goal: Convert Front customer emails into ClickUp feature requests with team notification
description: Customer feature requests received in Front are automatically triaged into ClickUp tasks and the product team is notified via Slack.

steps.action: Capture the tagged customer email and extract the feature request details
steps.error_hint: Front tags are workspace-specific — verify the tag_id matches the correct workspace before filtering

steps.action: Create a feature request task in the product backlog
steps.error_hint: ClickUp custom fields require the field_id, not the field name — look up IDs via the API first

steps.action: Notify the product team about the new feature request
steps.error_hint: Include the customer company name — enterprise requests get different prioritization than individual users

gotchas: Front conversations can have multiple messages — extract only the initial request, not the full thread, to avoid noise in the ClickUp task
Deduplicate by customer email + subject similarity to prevent the same request from creating multiple ClickUp tasks

goal: Safely deploy PlanetScale database schema changes via CI/CD
description: PlanetScale creates a schema branch for the migration, GitHub Actions runs tests against it, and a deploy request is created upon success.

steps.action: Create a PlanetScale branch with the schema changes
steps.error_hint: PlanetScale branch names must be unique — check for existing branches before creating to avoid naming conflicts

steps.action: Trigger the test workflow using the PlanetScale branch connection string
steps.error_hint: The workflow must use workflow_dispatch trigger — repository_dispatch has a different payload format

steps.action: Create a deploy request to merge the schema branch into production
steps.error_hint: Only create the deploy request if step 2 concluded with 'success' — failed tests should block the deploy

steps.action: Notify the team about the pending deploy request for review
steps.error_hint: Include a direct link to the deploy request diff — reviewers need to see the schema changes at a glance

gotchas: PlanetScale deploy requests require approval if safe migrations are enabled — the recipe cannot auto-merge without human review
Connection strings for PlanetScale branches are temporary — do not cache them; fetch fresh for each test run

goal: Generate monthly LLM cost reports from observability traces
description: Langfuse trace data is exported to BigQuery for cost aggregation, and a monthly summary is posted to Slack for budget tracking.

steps.action: Export Langfuse trace data via the API for the billing period
steps.error_hint: Langfuse API paginates large trace exports — implement cursor-based pagination to capture all traces in the period

steps.action: Insert traces and run cost aggregation query by model, project, and user
steps.error_hint: LLM pricing changes frequently — keep the pricing reference table updated or costs will be miscalculated

steps.action: Post the monthly LLM cost report to the finance/engineering channel
steps.error_hint: Format costs with currency symbols and 2 decimal places — raw floats are ambiguous

gotchas: Langfuse token counts may differ from provider billing — use Langfuse data for relative trends and provider invoices for absolute costs
BigQuery pricing table must account for different pricing tiers (e.g., input vs output tokens, cached vs non-cached)

goal: Automatically escalate support priority based on marketing lead scores
description: ActiveCampaign lead score changes trigger Freshdesk ticket priority updates and team alerts for high-value customers.

steps.action: Detect a lead score change event and retrieve the contact's current score and tier
steps.error_hint: ActiveCampaign webhooks fire on every score change — debounce to avoid processing rapid consecutive updates

steps.action: Update open ticket priorities for this customer based on their lead tier
steps.error_hint: Freshdesk priority values are integers (1=low to 4=urgent) — don't pass string labels

steps.action: Alert the support team lead about VIP customer ticket escalation
steps.error_hint: Only alert for tier upgrades to platinum/gold — alerting on every score change creates noise

gotchas: Lead score thresholds for tiers must match between ActiveCampaign automation rules and this recipe's tier mapping
Freshdesk ticket search by email may return tickets from different contacts at the same company domain — match on exact email, not domain

goal: Schedule and publish cross-platform social content with analytics tracking
description: Buffer schedules a post, which publishes to Twitter and YouTube Community, and engagement analytics are collected for reporting.

steps.action: Create and schedule a cross-platform social media post
steps.error_hint: Buffer profile_ids are specific to each connected account — verify IDs haven't changed after reconnecting accounts

steps.action: Fetch engagement metrics for the published tweet after a delay period
steps.error_hint: Twitter metrics API requires the tweet to be at least 15 minutes old — schedule the metrics fetch accordingly

steps.action: Fetch community post engagement metrics
steps.error_hint: YouTube Community posts API has limited metrics access — some engagement data may only be available via YouTube Studio

steps.action: Post the cross-platform analytics summary to the social team
steps.error_hint: Normalize metrics across platforms for fair comparison — Twitter impressions and YouTube views measure different things

gotchas: Buffer's update_id is not the same as the Twitter tweet_id — you need to fetch the published tweet ID from Buffer after posting
YouTube Community tab posts have limited API support — some operations may require scraping or manual verification

goal: Record enterprise payment captures in QuickBooks with team notification
description: Adyen captures a payment, QuickBooks records the journal entry for accounting, and Slack notifies the finance team.

steps.action: Capture the authorized payment and retrieve settlement details
steps.error_hint: Adyen capture requests can fail silently if the authorization has expired — check auth expiry before capturing

steps.action: Create a journal entry recording the payment in the general ledger
steps.error_hint: QuickBooks account IDs differ between sandbox and production — verify the account mapping after environment switches

steps.action: Notify the finance team about the recorded payment
steps.error_hint: Include the PSP reference in the Slack message so finance can cross-reference with the Adyen dashboard

gotchas: Adyen settlement amounts may differ from capture amounts due to fees — use the net settlement amount for the QuickBooks journal entry
Multi-currency payments require QuickBooks to have the currency enabled — unsupported currencies cause journal entry creation failure
